purpose: Shared Josh 2.0 / JAIMES model routing policy: model switching means checkpoint + fresh execution lane + verified model disclosure, not mutating the current session.
coreRule: A model switch request must launch a fresh lane/session using the requested provider/model, verify the lane before substantive work, then publish the active model/auth and route reason in Telegram and Brain Feed.

explicitOverridePolicy.rule: Josh's explicit model request overrides automatic routing unless the route is unsafe or unavailable.
unsafeExamples: Sending secrets, OAuth payloads, cookies, raw emails, private account contents, or raw connector/account data to Gemini, Grok/xAI, or OpenRouter
Using a read/review-only provider for irreversible external actions
Using metered API spend when Josh requested subscription-only routing
explicitOverridePolicy.unavailableBehavior: State the requested route was unavailable, explain the detected reason, then use the approved fallback lane.

steps: Checkpoint objective, repo path, branch/status, blockers, and last verified output.
Start a new session/lane with the chosen provider/model; do not resume the old session unless continuing the same model.
Verify the launch command or gateway response contains the requested model/provider before doing substantive work.
Make the first visible line Active Model/Auth plus a short route reason.
If verification fails, stop and report fallback options instead of pretending the switch happened.

telegramDisclosureTemplate.model: Model: <provider/model> - <requested by Josh | chosen by router>

codex.label: OpenAI Codex subscription
avoidFor: bulk public reading when Gemini is enough
X-native zeitgeist checks when Grok is available

gemini.label: Gemini CLI subscription
gemini.auth: Google Gemini CLI OAuth/subscription, not Google API key
avoidFor: secrets or raw private connector data

grok.label: Grok / xAI specialist
grok.auth: xAI/Grok host-local key or X Premium route when configured
avoidFor: repo mutations unless handed back to Codex

freshLaunch.note: Currently specialist-only; route remains unavailable until host-local xAI/Grok auth is installed.

openrouter.label: OpenRouter fallback/specialist
useFor: fallback when primary lanes are unavailable
